Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer of the barber shop, who is also a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the customer (matches Firebase Auth UID).

photoURL.description: URL of the customer's profile picture.

role.description: The user role in the system.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Represents a service offered by the barber shop.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents an appointment scheduled with a customer.

serviceIds.description: References to Services. (Relationship: Appointment N:N Service)

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Represents an invoice for services rendered.

paymentStatus.description: Payment status of the invoice (e.g., paid, unpaid, partially paid).

serviceIds.description: References to Services. (Relationship: Promotion N:N Service)

definition.description: Stores customer information. Each document represents a customer.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

definition.description: Stores appointments for a specific customer.  Path-based ownership: Each appointment is owned by a customer.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the appointment.

definition.description: Stores information about the services offered.  Accessible to all authenticated users.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the service.

definition.description: Stores invoices for a specific customer. Path-based ownership: Each invoice is owned by a customer.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the invoice.

definition.description: Stores information about promotions. Accessible to all authenticated users.

params.name: promotionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the promotion.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to manage data for a barber shop, focusing on customers, services, appointments, invoices, and promotions. It follows the principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling to ensure security and scalability. Authorization Independence is achieved by structuring data in a way that avoids the need for `get()` calls in security rules, using path-based ownership and denormalization where necessary.

Customers are stored under `/customers/{customerId}`, allowing for easy management of individual customer data. Appointments are stored under `/customers/{customerId}/appointments/{appointmentId}`, creating a clear ownership structure where appointments belong to customers. Services are stored in a top-level collection `/services/{serviceId}`, accessible to all authenticated users. Invoices are stored under `/customers/{customerId}/invoices/{invoiceId}`, maintaining the customer-invoice relationship. Promotions are stored under `/promotions/{promotionId}`, accessible to all authenticated users. This structure enables secure `list` operations by segregating data based on access needs and using path-based ownership.

This design ensures data integrity and simplifies security rules by making authorization context explicit and avoiding hierarchical dependencies.
